claim: A dog can survive approximately 72 hours (3 days) without water, but permanent organ damage can begin after just 24 hours of water deprivation.
stance_target: Water source gravity modeling — radius of fatal convergence by day 3 for lost dog in Algarve
scope_conditions: Healthy adult dogs; from veterinary source
quoted_support: Typically, your dog can survive without drinking water for about 72 hours (three days), but going longer than 24 hours without water could lead to permanent organ damage.

claim: A dog requires approximately 1 ounce of water per pound of body weight per day (a 25 lb dog needs ~25 oz/3 cups; a 75 lb dog needs ~2 liters).
stance_target: Quantifying water deficit accumulation and forced convergence timeline for lost dog search
scope_conditions: Healthy adult dogs at rest in normal conditions
quoted_support: A good guideline for dogs is about an ounce of water for every pound of body weight. So, a 25-lb dog needs about 25 oz. of water (or 3 cups), while a 75-lb dog needs almost 2 liters of water daily.
numbers: 1 oz water per pound body weight per day

claim: Hot and humid climate accelerates water loss through panting; more heat means more panting, which means faster water depletion and earlier dehydration.
stance_target: Algarve summer heat as an accelerant of water-source convergence behavior in lost dogs
scope_conditions: Dogs in hot/humid environments vs. mild climates
quoted_support: If you live in a mild climate, your dog will be fine without water for longer than that same dog living in a hot, dry, or humid environment. More heat means more panting, which means more water loss.

claim: Dehydration signs appear within the first 24 hours — including dry/tacky gums, low skin elasticity (skin-tent test), dark yellow urine, sunken eyes, lethargy, and loss of appetite.
stance_target: Field assessment of found dog dehydration state to estimate time since last water source
quoted_support: your dog will show signs of dehydration after the first 24 hours... Dry, tacky gums and pasty saliva... Low skin elasticity... Sunken, dry eyes.

claim: Water makes up more than 70% of a dog's body weight, making hydration the single most critical survival variable — above food.
stance_target: Priority ranking of water vs. food stations in lost dog search infrastructure
quoted_support: Water makes up more than 70% of your dog's body by weight, so your dog needs to maintain this water content by consuming plenty daily.
